name: Guainia (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the gold and other mineral wealth of the territory
the great rivers (Guainia, Inirida, Atabapo and Negro) that traverse the department
the dense Amazonian rainforest covering most of Guainia

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of yellow, blue, and green.
history: Guainia was created as a Comisaria in 1963 and elevated to a department by the 1991 Constitution. The name 'Guainia' is from an indigenous word meaning 'land of many waters', reflected in the central blue stripe of the flag.

features.symbolism: yellow for the gold and natural wealth, blue for the great rivers of the region (Guainia, Inirida, Atabapo, Negro), and green for the surrounding Amazonian rainforest
